prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan (9 atoms: O C C C C H H H H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit RKS) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 for the neutral species (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization at DFT level). All three SCFs converged. Fukui charge-conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000). Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true.

The headline value reported is the largest f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index), 1.9242931, located on atom 5 (C, 1-based). The same atom also carries the largest f⁻ = 0.1616371. The α-carbons of furan (C4 and C5) are identified as the most reactive sites toward both electrophilic and nucleophilic attack, consistent with the known reactivity of furan.

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz
